title: Dictionaries and sets deterministic draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and runnable core task for Dictionaries and sets; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Dictionaries and sets: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Sets and dictionaries sort their contents automatically and any Python object can be used safely as a key.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use stable hashable keys, distinguish insertion order from sorted order, and handle missing-key behavior deliberately with indexing, get, or setdefault.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Use stable hashable keys, distinguish insertion order from sorted order, and handle missing-key behavior deliberately with indexing, get, or setdefault.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Use stable hashable keys, distinguish insertion order from sorted order, and handle missing-key behavior deliberately with indexing, get, or setdefault.
Sets and dictionaries sort their contents automatically and any Python object can be used safely as a key.

answer.explanation: Use stable hashable keys, distinguish insertion order from sorted order, and handle missing-key behavior deliberately with indexing, get, or setdefault.

items.title: Dictionaries and sets: canonical checkpoint
items.prompt: Complete the source-aligned Python: Beginner to Intermediate correction with the exact canonical phrase used by this lesson.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'stable hashable keys'. Use stable hashable keys, distinguish insertion order from sorted order, and handle missing-key behavior deliberately with indexing, get, or setdefault.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the exact language rule. Use stable hashable keys, distinguish insertion order from sorted order, and handle missing-key behavior deliberately with indexing, get, or setdefault.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical checkpoint phrase that completes the source-aligned correction.

items.title: Dictionaries and sets: runnable core task
items.prompt: Read words. Count them with a dictionary and print sorted key=count pairs, followed by the sorted unique words.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Start from the stated input/output contract, implement the smallest language-specific operation, and run the visible boundary before changing structure.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden Python: Beginner to Intermediate runner checks match the declared contract.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic runner check differs; inspect input parsing, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Compiles or parses and passes every visible and hidden deterministic runner check for the declared contract.

privateAuthorNotes: Reference solution and both tests are AI-assisted draft material. Execute in the pinned runner and obtain independent human review before any exam use.

answer.explanation: The dictionary provides keyed counting, the set provides uniqueness, and sorting is requested explicitly for deterministic presentation.
